Reduce ORG-panel clutter by limiting how many agents the cockpit shows (the agent table accumulates every historical agent). Work ONLY in sidecar/src/server.js and sidecar/test/console-parser.test.mjs. domain=backend. (1) Add an exported pure helper selectVisibleAgents(agents, cap=16) that returns at most `cap` agents, preferring agents whose status === 'working' first, then the remaining agents in their input order, returning each agent object unchanged. (2) In snapshot(), keep querying ALL agents, set agentTotal = the full count, then set agents = selectVisibleAgents(allAgents, 16); include agentTotal in the returned object alongside the now-capped agents array. Do NOT change or remove any other snapshot field, route, or behavior. (3) In sidecar/test/console-parser.test.mjs add assertions (keep ALL existing ones): selectVisibleAgents of an array of 20 objects {status:'idle'} returns length 16; and selectVisibleAgents([{status:'working',id:'w'}, ...nineteen {status:'idle'}], 3) returns length 3 with the first element having status 'working'. DONE WHEN: 'node sidecar/test/console-parser.test.mjs' prints PASS, AND 'node --check sidecar/src/server.js' exits 0, AND (integration check) starting the cockpit on a free port — in PowerShell `$env:COCKPIT_PORT=4405; node --no-warnings sidecar/scripts/cockpit.js` in the background — then `Invoke-RestMethod http://localhost:4405/api/state` returns JSON whose `agents` array length is <= 16 and whose `agentTotal` is a number; then stop that server. Put the integration-check result in your report.

Make the cockpit render INCREMENTALLY so it stops rebuilding the entire DOM on every SSE update (it currently re-renders all panels each tick, which is sluggish with many agents/events). Work ONLY in sidecar/web/ (app.js, plus index.html/style.css only if a tiny change is needed). In the render(s) function, compute a cheap per-panel signature (e.g. JSON.stringify of just that panel's relevant data slice) for each panel: stepper (cycleState), the header readouts (goal/spend/metrics/killSwitch), agents, kanban (tickets), changes (changes+approvals), feed (events), console, and chat. Store the last signature per panel in module scope and ONLY rebuild a panel's DOM when its signature changed since the last render — an unchanged SSE tick must perform ZERO innerHTML writes. Also, in the ORG panel heading show '(N of agentTotal)' using /api/state agentTotal when present (N = number of agents shown). Preserve ALL element ids and the EventSource('/events') flow; the live data must still update correctly. Use the impeccable skill for any visual touch. domain=frontend. DONE WHEN: 'node --check sidecar/web/app.js' exits 0 AND sidecar/web/index.html still contains the ids agents, kanban, changes, feed, console, chat, cyclesteps.
